detaileddescription.para: Stores a functor of type: void(MR::UndirectedEdgeId, const MR::Vector2f &, float). Possibly stateful. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

Stores a functor of type: void(MR::UndirectedEdgeId, const MR::Vector3f &, float). Possibly stateful. Supported MR_PassBy modes: MR_PassBy_DefaultConstruct, MR_PassBy_Copy, MR_PassBy_Move (and MR_PassBy_DefaultArgument and MR_PassBy_NoObject if supported by the callee).

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Vector2f *pt, const MR_Polyline2 *polyline, const float *upDistLimitSq, MR_AffineXf2f *xf, const float *loDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findProjectionOnPolyline2

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on polyline to given point

parameterdescription.para: upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid point or edge
polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findProjectionOnPolyline2. Parameter pt can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimitSq has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PolylineProjectionResult2_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Vector3f *pt, const MR_Polyline3 *polyline, const float *upDistLimitSq, MR_AffineXf3f *xf, const float *loDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findProjectionOnPolyline_MR_Vector3f

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on polyline to given point

parameterdescription.para: upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid point or edge
polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findProjectionOnPolyline. Parameter pt can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimitSq has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PolylineProjectionResult3_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

Upcasts an instance of MR::PolylineProjectionResult3Arg to its base class MR::PolylineProjectionResult3. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::PolylineProjectionResult3 to a derived class MR::PolylineProjectionResult3Arg. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. This version is acting on mutable pointers. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

Downcasts an instance of MR::PolylineProjectionResult3 to a derived class MR::PolylineProjectionResult3Arg. This is a static downcast, it trusts the programmer that the target type is correct. Results in UB and returns an invalid pointer otherwise. The reference to the parameter object might be preserved in the return value.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_VertCoords *points, const MR_Polyline3 *polyline, const MR_VertBitSet *pointsRegion, MR_AffineXf3f *xf, const float *loDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findMaxProjectionOnPolyline

briefdescription.para: for each of points (pointsRegion) computes the closest point on polyline and returns the point for which maximum distance is reached,

parameterdescription.para: polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findMaxProjectionOnPolyline. Parameter points can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter pointsRegion defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimitSq has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PolylineProjectionResult3Arg_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.argsstring: (const MR_Line3f *ln, const MR_Polyline3 *polyline, const float *upDistLimitSq, MR_AffineXf3f *xf, const float *loDistLimitSq)
memberdef.name: MR_findProjectionOnPolyline_MR_Line3f

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on polyline to given straight line

parameterdescription.para: upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid point or edge
polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findProjectionOnPolyline. Parameter ln can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimitSq has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PolylineProjectionResult3_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

memberdef.name: MR_findProjectionOnPolyline2WithOffset

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on polyline to given point, respecting each edge offset

parameterdescription.para: offset for each edge of polyline
upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimit and no valid point or edge
polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findProjectionOnPolyline2WithOffset. Parameter pt can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter offsetPerEdge can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter upDistLimit has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimit has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_Polyline2ProjectionWithOffsetResult_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on polyline to given point, respecting each edge offset

parameterdescription.para: offset for each edge of polyline

polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

detaileddescription.para: Finds all edges of given polyline that cross or touch given ball (center, radius) Generated from function MR::findEdgesInBall. Parameter polyline can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter center can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter foundCallback can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++.

memberdef.name: MR_findProjectionOnMeshEdges_MR_Vector3f

briefdescription.para: computes the closest point on the mesh edges (specified by the tree) to given point

parameterdescription.para: upper limit on the distance in question, if the real distance is larger then the function exists returning upDistLimitSq and no valid point or edge
polyline-to-point transformation, if not specified then identity transformation is assumed

low limit on the distance in question, if a point is found within this distance then it is immediately returned without searching for a closer one Generated from function MR::findProjectionOnMeshEdges. Parameter pt can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter mesh can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter tree can not be null. It is a single object. Parameter upDistLimitSq has a default argument: 3.40282347e38f, pass a null pointer to use it. Parameter xf defaults to a null pointer in C++. Parameter loDistLimitSq has a default argument: 0, pass a null pointer to use it. Never returns null. Returns an instance allocated on the heap! Must call MR_PolylineProjectionResult3_Destroy() to free it when you're done using it.
